heading: Definitions
label: 7412 Definitions
notes: Amendments2014—Par. (1)(E) to (G). added subpar. (E) and redesignated former subpars. (E) and (F) as (F) and (G), respectively.Pub. L. 113–79
amendments: Amendments2014—Par. (1)(E) to (G). added subpar. (E) and redesignated former subpars. (E) and (F) as (F) and (G), respectively.Pub. L. 113–79

children.label: A
children.text: agricultural, horticultural, viticultural, and dairy products;

children.label: B
children.text: livestock and the products of livestock;

children.label: C
children.text: the products of poultry and bee raising;

children.label: D
children.text: the products of forestry;

children.label: i
children.text: produced on a certified organic farm (as defined in ); andsection 6502 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: certified to be sold or labeled as “organic” or “100 percent organic” (as defined in part 205 of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation));

children.label: F
children.text: other commodities raised or produced on farms, as determined appropriate by the Secretary; and

children.label: G
children.text: products processed or manufactured from products specified in the preceding subparagraphs, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 2 Board
children.text: The term “board” means a board established under an order issued under .section 7413 of this title

children.heading: Conflict of interest
children.label: 3 Conflict of interest
children.text: The term “conflict of interest” means a situation in which a member or employee of a board has a direct or indirect financial interest in a person that performs a service for, or enters into a contract with, a board for anything of economic value.

children.heading: Department
children.label: 4 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: First handler
children.label: 5 First handler
children.text: The term “first handler” means the first person who buys or takes possession of an agricultural commodity from a producer for marketing. If a producer markets the agricultural commodity directly to consumers, the producer shall be considered to be the first handler with respect to the agricultural commodity produced by the producer.

children.heading: Importer
children.label: 6 Importer
children.text: The term “importer” means any person who imports an agricultural commodity from outside the United States for sale in the United States as a principal or as an agent, broker, or consignee of any person.

children.label: A
children.text: efficiency in processing; and

children.label: B
children.text: the development of new markets, marketing strategies, increased marketing efficiency, and activities to enhance the image of agricultural commodities on a national or international basis.

children.heading: Market
children.label: 8 Market
children.text: The term “market” means to sell or to otherwise dispose of an agricultural commodity in interstate, foreign, or intrastate commerce.

children.label: A
children.text: strengthen the position of agricultural commodity industries in the marketplace;

children.label: B
children.text: maintain and expand existing domestic and foreign markets and uses for agricultural commodities;

children.label: C
children.text: develop new markets and uses for agricultural commodities; or

children.label: D
children.text: assist producers in meeting their conservation objectives.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 10 Person
children.text: The term “person” means any individual, group of individuals, partnership, corporation, association, cooperative, or any other legal entity.

children.heading: Producer
children.label: 11 Producer
children.text: The term “producer” means any person who is engaged in the production and sale of an agricultural commodity in the United States and who owns, or shares the ownership and risk of loss of, the agricultural commodity.

children.heading: Promotion
children.label: 12 Promotion
children.text: The term “promotion” means any action taken by a board under an order, including paid advertising, to present a favorable image of an agricultural commodity to the public to improve the competitive position of the agricultural commodity in the marketplace and to stimulate sales of the agricultural commodity.

children.heading: Research
children.label: 13 Research
children.text: The term “research” means any type of test, study, or analysis designed to advance the image, desirability, use, marketability, production, product development, or quality of an agricultural commodity.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 14 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: State
children.label: 15 State
children.text: The term “State” means any of the States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, or any territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Suspend
children.label: 16 Suspend
children.text: The term “suspend” means to issue a rule under to temporarily prevent the operation of an order during a particular period of time specified in the rule.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Terminate
children.label: 17 Terminate
children.text: The term “terminate” means to issue a rule under to cancel permanently the operation of an order beginning on a date certain specified in the rule.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: United States
children.label: 18 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means collectively the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico and the territories and possessions of the United States.
